reasoning: The question asks if the total C&I loans value (FRED series TOTCI) will be strictly higher on the resolution dates compared to its baseline on Forecast Date (2026-03-01). The latest published value as of March 1 is for the week ending Feb 25, which sits at 2787.04. This represents an enormous local spike (it jumped +33 in the week of Feb 4 and has since flatlined/slightly declined). The linear trend predicts a value closer to 2763 for early March, suggesting the current level is an anomalous spike (perhaps a large corporate facility drawdown) subject to mean reversion over the coming weeks. The tool estimates a 31% chance of exceeding the 2787 threshold in the near term. For the Mar 8 resolution, mean reversion likely keeps the value lower than the current peak. By Mar 31, the underlying upward drift (approx +15/month) will pull the baseline expectation closer to the threshold, pushing the probability back toward 0.5. Therefore, early March is less likely to see an increase, with uncertainty increasing (probability moving closer to 50%) for the later March target.
